decks.notes: The deck focuses on aggressive creatures such as Amped Raptor, Guide of Souls, and Voice of Victory to quickly pressure opponents. It also includes disruptive elements like Thoughtseize, Cabal Therapy, and Wasteland to control the opponent's resources. The inclusion of cards like Goblin Bombardment and Ajani, Nacatl Pariah provide additional reach and ways to close out games. Overall, the deck aims to apply early pressure, disrupt the opponent's game plan, and finish with powerful threats to secure victory.

The key strategy of this deck is to quickly assemble and cast the Doomsday card to set up a winning combo. Using efficient mana acceleration and disruption spells like Force of Will, the deck aims to control the game while searching for its combo pieces. Once Doomsday is cast, the player can carefully stack their deck to ensure a win condition, typically involving Thassa's Oracle or a large creature like Murktide Regent. The deck also includes card draw and searching effects to help find crucial cards and maintain consistency in executing the combo efficiently.

The main idea behind this deck is to generate a large amount of spells and mana quickly to achieve a storm count high enough to cast Tendrils of Agony and win the game. The deck uses cards like Dark Ritual, Lotus Petal, and Lion's Eye Diamond to generate mana, while also utilizing cards like Brainstorm and Gamble to find the necessary pieces for the combo. Burning Wish serves as a versatile tool to access key combo pieces from the sideboard, allowing the deck to adapt to different matchups and situations. The goal is to quickly assemble the necessary components for a lethal Storm combo and overwhelm the opponent before they can react.

The deck aims to quickly combo off by milling the entire deck with cards like Balustrade Spy and Undercity Informer to put all creatures into the graveyard. Then, using Dread Return to reanimate Thassa's Oracle and win the game on the spot. The deck utilizes fast mana sources like Dark Ritual and Lotus Petal, as well as free spells like Pact of Negation and Simian Spirit Guide to power out its game-winning combo as quickly as possible while disrupting opponents with Cabal Therapy and Chancellor of the Annex.

This Boros Aggro deck focuses on quickly overwhelming opponents with efficient creatures and removal spells. Utilizing a mix of aggressive creatures like Amped Raptor and Sand Scout, along with powerful planeswalkers like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah, the deck aims to apply constant pressure while disrupting opponents' game plans with cards like Swords to Plowshares and Hexing Squelcher. The inclusion of Goblin Bombardment provides extra reach and versatility, while cards like Wasteland and Karakas help control the board and protect key threats. Overall, this deck aims to win fast and efficiently through a mix of aggressive creatures and disruption.

The deck focuses on assembling the Painter's Servant and Grindstone combo to mill the opponent's entire library. It uses cards like Goblin Welder and Goblin Engineer to find and protect the combo pieces, while utilizing powerful artifact cards like Mox Opal and Lotus Petal for mana acceleration. The deck also includes disruptive elements like Pyroblast and Ensnaring Bridge to control the game and protect the key combo. The main goal is to quickly set up the combo and win the game before the opponent can find a way to disrupt it.

The main concept of this deck is to quickly and efficiently cheat out powerful creatures like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn using cards like Show and Tell and Sneak Attack. By utilizing cards such as Atraxa, Grand Unifier and Omniscience, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with massive threats while also maintaining control through counterspells like Force of Will and Spell Pierce. The deck also includes cards like Brainstorm and Ponder for card selection and manipulation, as well as acceleration through Lotus Petal and Ancient Tomb. The goal is to win the game by quickly putting a game-ending threat into play before the opponent can react effectively.

The deck utilizes cards like Show and Tell and Sneak Attack to cheat powerful creatures like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn and Archon of Cruelty into play quickly and unexpectedly. The goal is to overwhelm the opponent with these massive threats before they have a chance to respond. The deck also includes counterspells like Force of Will and Daze to protect its game-winning combos. By efficiently ramping and controlling the game, the deck aims to bring out its powerful creatures and quickly close out the game.

The main concept of this Combo - Oops, All Spells! deck is to quickly and efficiently mill through the entire library using cards like Balustrade Spy and Undercity Informer to put all creatures into the graveyard. Then, using cards like Reanimate and Dread Return to bring back powerful creatures like Thassa's Oracle to win the game instantly. The deck utilizes a lot of fast mana cards like Lotus Petal and Simian Spirit Guide to accelerate the combo. Cabal Therapy and Pact of Negation are also included to disrupt opponents' plans and protect key combo pieces.

The deck revolves around quickly putting big creatures onto the battlefield using Show and Tell and Sneak Attack, in order to overwhelm the opponent and win the game in a single turn. It utilizes powerful spells like Brainstorm, Ponder, and Force of Will to manipulate the game state and protect its combos. The inclusion of Atraxa, Grand Unifier and Omniscience adds versatility and resilience to the strategy, while cards like Yggdrasil, Rebirth Engine and Raph & Mikey, Troublemakers provide additional disruptive elements to control the game. Ultimately, the goal is to assemble the combo pieces and execute a game-winning play before the opponent can react.

This deck focuses on fast-paced aggression and disruption, utilizing efficient creatures like Dauthi Voidwalker and Nethergoyf to apply pressure on the opponent. It also includes disruptive spells like Thoughtseize and Fatal Push to control the opponent's resources. The deck's mana base is optimized for consistency and speed, allowing for quick deployment of threats. The inclusion of cards like Moonshadow and Super Shredder provide additional advantages and flexibility in combat situations. Overall, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a combination of early aggression and disruption.

This deck focuses on using Aether Vial to quickly put creatures into play and apply pressure on the opponent. It also utilizes disruptive cards like Force of Will and Meddling Mage to control the game and protect its creatures. The inclusion of Yorion, Sky Nomad as a companion provides additional value and flexibility. The deck aims to maintain a strong board presence while disrupting the opponent's game plan, ultimately overwhelming them with a combination of aggression and control elements.

This combo deck focuses on generating a large amount of mana quickly to cast spells that draw cards, copy spells, and manipulate the opponent's hand. The goal is to set up a large storm count through spells like Dark Ritual, Lion's Eye Diamond, and Burning Wish, then finish the game with a powerful Tendrils of Agony. The deck also includes disruption elements like Silence and Hexing Squelcher to protect the combo and disrupt the opponent's strategy. The use of cards like Beseech the Mirror and Echo of Eons allow for flexibility and card advantage in different situations.

The deck focuses on assembling the Painter's Servant and Grindstone combo to mill the opponent's deck. It also utilizes cards like Goblin Welder and Urza's Saga to generate mana and find key pieces. Emry, Lurker of the Loch helps with recurring artifacts while cards like Stock Up and Thoughtcast provide card draw. Force of Will offers protection against opposing spells. Overall, the deck aims to quickly set up the combo and disrupt the opponent's game plan to achieve victory.

The deck focuses on utilizing powerful control elements such as Swords to Plowshares, Force of Will, and Teferi, Time Raveler to disrupt opponents' strategies while generating card advantage through Brainstorm, Ponder, and Quantum Riddler. It also includes efficient removal spells like Prismatic Ending and flexible threats like Murktide Regent and Uro, Titan of Nature's Wrath. The mana base is designed to support multiple colors, ensuring access to key spells and utilizing cards like Wasteland for resource denial. Overall, the deck aims to control the game's pace and outvalue opponents through strategic decision-making and efficient card selection.

The deck focuses on efficiently utilizing aggressive creatures and combat tricks to overwhelm the opponent quickly. It includes powerful cards like Ajani, Guide of Souls, and Voice of Victory to boost the damage output of the creatures. It also includes disruptive elements like Hexing Squelcher and Static Prison to control the opponent's board. The mana base is carefully constructed to support the aggro strategy, with fetch lands and dual lands to ensure consistent access to various colors. Overall, the deck aims to put pressure on the opponent early and maintain that pressure throughout the game.
